reason: xsalsa20poly1305 renamed to crypto_secretbox upstream. btt uses xsalsa20poly1305 as a direct dep for the wallet key envelope. Migration tracked separately; suppress advisory until then. Note: the original pre-#11 reason field incorrectly attributed this id to bigdecimal
unmaintained: paste (still transitively pulled in post-subxt-0.50); no runtime-exploitable surface; remaining after ErgodicLabs/btt#11 phase A

unmaintained: libsecp256k1 (still transitively pulled in post-subxt-0.50 via polkadot-sdk dev chain); no runtime-exploitable surface in btt (no secp256k1 signing path); remaining after ErgodicLabs/btt#11 phase A

unsound: lru 0.12.5 — `IterMut` violates Stacked Borrows by invalidating an internal pointer (rustsec.org title: 'IterMut violates Stacked Borrows by invalidating internal pointer'). Transitively pulled in via smoldot-light -> subxt-lightclient -> subxt 0.50 -> btt. No runtime-exploitable surface: btt source does not instantiate `lru::LruCache` or call `IterMut` on any lru cache (grep of src/ is clean). Suppression stays until subxt's light-client stack drops or updates lru.

unmaintained: derivative 2.2.0 (rustsec.org title: 'derivative is unmaintained; consider using an alternative'). Transitively pulled in via ark-ec / ark-ff / ark-poly -> ark-bls12-377 -> w3f-bls -> sp-core 40 -> btt. No runtime-exploitable surface; `derivative` is a proc-macro for #[derive(...)] expansions only. Suppression stays until the arkworks/w3f-bls/sp-core chain drops derivative.

unsound: rand 0.8.5 with a custom logger using rand::rng() (transitive via jsonrpsee/subxt). btt does not install a custom logger; no runtime-exploitable surface. Note: the original pre-#11 reason field incorrectly attributed this id to the instant crate
